It is a type of bramble, a deciduous shrub with thorny stems and edible fruits. As a subspecies of Rubus schleicheri, Rubus schleicheri microgene apertiflorus shares many characteristics with its parent species, including its habit of producing white or pink flowers and red or purple berries. At the species level, Rubus schleicheri microgene apertiflorus is a member of the genus Rubus, which contains many other species of brambles and raspberries.
